explicit_text: My parents and all my ancestors come from China, and they made the difficult decision to leave because they felt there was no freedom or democracy there. My dad is from Beijing, the capital, and my mom is from Shandong province in eastern China. Even though my mom was an engineer with a master's degree in China, she had to start over when we moved. China is where our roots are, but my family sought better opportunities abroad.
implicit_text: My parents and all my ancestors come from a large East Asian country, and they made the difficult decision to leave because they felt there was no freedom or democracy there. My dad is from the capital city, and my mom is from a province in the eastern part of that country. Even though my mom was an engineer with a master's degree there, she had to start over when we moved. That country is where our roots are, but my family sought better opportunities abroad.

explicit_text: Canada is where my parents decided to build a new life for our family, seeking better opportunities and more freedom. They first arrived in 2002, and over the years we lived in several Canadian cities like Toronto, Montreal, Richmond Hill, and Vaughan. Canada was welcoming but challenging, especially since my parents had to adapt professionally and linguistically. It’s been the backdrop of our immigrant journey and growth.
implicit_text: This North American country is where my parents decided to build a new life for our family, seeking better opportunities and more freedom. They first arrived in 2002, and over the years we lived in several cities like Toronto, Montreal, Richmond Hill, and Vaughan. The country was welcoming but challenging, especially since my parents had to adapt professionally and linguistically. It’s been the backdrop of our immigrant journey and growth.

explicit_text: Toronto was the first city my parents settled in when they came to Canada because they had friends there. Many Chinese immigrants choose Toronto due to its large Chinese community, which made the transition easier. It was in Toronto that my brother Tristan was born in 2004 during our journey. Toronto marked the beginning of our life in Canada.

explicit_text: Vancouver is one of the popular destinations for Chinese immigrants in Canada, similar to Toronto. Although my family didn’t live there, I know many Chinese immigrants settle in Vancouver because of its big Chinese community. It was part of the context when my parents chose Toronto and Montreal for our family’s settlement.
implicit_text: This city on the west coast of that North American country is one of the popular destinations for immigrants from my parents' homeland, similar to the largest city on the east side. Although my family didn’t live there, I know many immigrants from that background settle in this west coast city because of its big community from their culture. It was part of the context when my parents chose the largest city and the French-speaking city for our family’s settlement.

explicit_text: After Toronto, my family moved to Montreal so my parents and my brother could get a better education. My mom studied at McGill University there, learning French as part of her program. My brother even went to a French daycare in Montreal, making French his first language, although his current French grades are low. Living in Montreal meant adapting to a mandatory French-speaking environment.

explicit_text: My mom was offered a job at the University of Florida, which led our family to move to Gainesville for about a year. It was during that time in Gainesville that I was born. This move was a brief but important chapter in our journey, connecting our Canadian and American experiences.
implicit_text: My mom was offered a job at a major public university in a southeastern U.S. state, which led our family to move to the university's city for about a year. It was during that time in this city that I was born. This move was a brief but important chapter in our journey, connecting our Canadian and American experiences.

explicit_text: Gainesville, Florida is where our family lived for a year when my mom worked at the University of Florida. It’s also the place where I was born. This was a unique part of our story, bridging our time between Canada and the U.S.

explicit_text: In 2008, my family moved to Richmond Hill, Ontario, mainly for real estate reasons. This was a more permanent step in settling down in Canada after our time in the U.S. Richmond Hill became our home before we later moved to Vaughan.

explicit_text: After Richmond Hill, we moved to Vaughan, Ontario, where we currently live. Vaughan has become our family’s stable home as we continue to build our lives in Canada. It’s where we have settled after many moves across cities.

explicit_text: My mom had to study again at McGill University in Montreal because even though she was an engineer in China, she needed to reapply for her license in Canada. McGill was where she learned French and retrained to continue her professional career. It was a tough but necessary step in adapting to life in Canada.
implicit_text: My mom had to study again at a prestigious university in the French-speaking city because even though she was an engineer in her home country, she needed to reapply for her license in this new country. This university was where she learned French and retrained to continue her professional career. It was a tough but necessary step in adapting to life here.

explicit_text: My mom currently works as a librarian at York University, which is quite different from her previous engineering career in China. This job represents how she adapted and found new roles in Canada after immigrating. York University is part of our family's Canadian experience.
